incorrects.description: Student placed incorrect tiles on first attempt

steps.text: Look closely at the bags. Count how many bags, then count what's in each bag.

incorrects.description: Student placed incorrect tiles on second attempt

params.description: The 9 bags highlight. Then one bag highlights individually, showing the 5 items inside clearly.

steps.text: Let's think about this together. You have 9 bags. Each bag contains 5 items. That's 9 groups of 5. Skip count by 5s nine times to find the product. Place all three tiles.

incorrects.description: Student placed incorrect tiles three or more times. System models the answer.

params.description: System places 9 in first slot, 5 in second slot, 45 in product slot. Each tile locks into position sequentially.

steps.text: Let me show you how this works. I count the bags: 9 bags. That's my first factor. I count what's in each bag: 5 items. That's my second factor. Now I skip count by 5s, nine times: 5, 10, 15, 20, 25, 30, 35, 40, 45. The product is 45. The complete equation is 9 times 5 equals 45.
